description: The presentation vocabulary (SPEC §2 L2). Every screen in every game is an instance of a registered scene type rendering a slice of game state. m8-engine-smith implements the types a GDD activates; m8-menu-wright owns UX conventions within them. Extend by RFC.

scene_types.notes: tile movement, encounter rolls, entity triggers

scene_types.state_slice: battle state (parties, rows, turn order, statuses)
scene_types.notes: FF-style menu battle; turn engine per GDD (v0.1: round-based, agility-ordered)
FF1-style start-of-game party creation; GDD cast_model=player_built
JSON snapshot round-trip is a build-warden gate

ux_conventions.cursor_memory: each menu remembers last cursor position within a scene visit
ux_conventions.cancel_semantics: cancel always steps back exactly one level; never destructive without confirm
ux_conventions.text_speed: instant in v0.1; per-character reveal is an M3 polish item
